Prism Analytics Labs // Staff Data Engineer

San Francisco, CA (Remote-First) | Full-time | $240,000 - $320,000 USD annually

Building the data foundation for intelligent enterprises

>> ABOUT

Prism Analytics Labs is a venture-backed data infrastructure company founded in 2018 that
helps Fortune 500 enterprises build scalable, real-time data platforms. We serve over 200

clients across finance, healthcare, and e-commerce, processing over 10 petabytes of data
daily. Our team of 150+ engineers focuses on solving complex data challenges through modern

cloud-native technologies and best-in-class engineering practices.

>> SUMMARY

Prism Analytics Labs is seeking an exceptionally skilled Staff Data Engineer to join our

Platform Engineering team. You will provide technical leadership across multiple
engineering squads, drive architectural decisions for our data infrastructure, and mentor a

diverse group of data engineers. This role reports directly to our VP of Engineering and
requires deep expertise in distributed systems, data warehousing, and ETL orchestration.

You'll own the evolution of our core data platform serving 200+ enterprise clients.

>> RESPONSIBILITIES

- Lead the design and implementation of scalable data architecture patterns across 5+

engineering teams, ensuring consistency, reliability, and performance at petabyte scale
- Drive technical strategy for real-time data ingestion using Kafka and stream

processing, establishing best practices and patterns adopted across the organization
- Mentor and develop 8-10 mid-level and senior data engineers, conducting code reviews,

designing interview questions, and providing regular feedback on technical growth
- Own the migration of legacy ETL systems (built on custom tooling) to dbt + Airflow,

leading cross-functional efforts and reducing deployment friction by 70%+
- Architect and implement advanced data modeling solutions in Snowflake/BigQuery for

high-cardinality customer datasets, optimizing query performance and cost efficiency
- Establish observability and monitoring standards for data pipelines using metrics,

alerts, and SLOs; maintain 99.95% pipeline reliability across the platform
- Partner with product leadership and customers on strategic data platform roadmap,

influencing quarterly OKRs and technical investment priorities
- Conduct deep-dives into critical production incidents, document root causes, and

implement systematic improvements to prevent recurrence

>> MUST HAVE

- 8+ years of data engineering experience with 3+ years in a staff, principal, or similar

technical leadership role
- Deep expertise with Apache Spark and distributed computing concepts; proven experience

optimizing large-scale batch and streaming workloads
- Expert-level proficiency in Python and SQL; comfortable writing performant, production-

grade data transformation logic

 - Hands-on experience building and maintaining data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, or

Redshift); deep understanding of dimensional modeling, schema design, and query
optimization

- Proven experience architecting and deploying ETL orchestration systems using Airflow or
equivalent (Dagster, Prefect); demonstrated ability to scale orchestration to 1000+ daily

DAGs
- Strong background in data modeling and implementation; experience leading data modeling

discussions and resolving complex schema conflicts
- Exceptional communication skills and track record of mentoring engineers; demonstrated

ability to influence stakeholders across technical and non-technical teams

>> NICE TO HAVE

- Experience with dbt and modern data stack tooling; expertise in building reusable dbt

macros, tests, and data quality frameworks
- Production experience with Apache Kafka or similar event streaming platforms;

understanding of stream processing trade-offs and consistency guarantees
- Background in data governance, data cataloging (e.g., Collibra, DataHub), or metadata

management at scale
- Track record of leading architectural initiatives or RFCs that shaped data platform

direction
- Open-source contributions to data infrastructure projects (Spark, Airflow, dbt, or

similar)

>> STACK

Apache Spark / Apache Airflow / dbt (data build tool) / Snowflake / Python / SQL / Apache Kafka /

BigQuery / Data modeling and schema design / Kubernetes (for orchestration infrastructure)

>> BENEFITS

- Competitive salary + equity package (0.5-1.2% depending on experience)

- Comprehensive health coverage: medical, dental, vision with 90% premiums covered
- Unlimited PTO with minimum 20 days encouraged; additional parental leave (16 weeks)

- Professional development budget of $5,000 annually; conference attendance and training
support

- Stock options with 4-year vesting and 1-year cliff; equity refresh grants for high
performers

- Flexible work environment: remote-first with optional access to SF office; $1,500 home
office stipend

- Mental health and wellness: on-site gym membership, meditation app access, quarterly
all-hands offsite

- Learning stipend and Databricks/Snowflake certification support; internal tech talks
and brown-bag sessions

>> APPLY

Apply directly via our careers page at prismanalyticslabs.com/careers or email your resume
and cover letter to careers@prismanalyticslabs.com. Include links to GitHub profile or

public data engineering work samples if available. We review applications on a rolling
basis and move quickly for exceptional candidates.
